name: Dutch Latrine Bag
description: The Dutch Latrine Bag is a large plastic latrine pit liner which prevents human waste from polluting the groundwater table.

taxonomy.sub_category: Dutch Latrine Bag discontinued Updated on December 19, 2023 · Created on October 1, 2018 Dutch Latrine Bag Discontinued The Dutch Latrine Bag is a large plastic latrine pit liner which prevents human waste from polluting the groundwater table. Developed By Aqua-Aero WaterSystems BV (AAWS) Tested By Aqua-Aero WaterSystems BV (AAWS) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Discontinued Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Dutch Latrine Bag is a large, strong plastic bag used to line latrine pits. The bag both prevents human waste from contaminating the groundwater table, and allows for the waste to be more easily recycled for agricultural or biogas applications. Pit latrines can be alternatively lined with brick and mortar, concrete, sandbags, or timber. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Community, Public Sector Agencies, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations The bags are distributed directly from the manufacturer. Regions Africa Manufacturing/Building Method The Dutch Latrine Bags are manufactured using a thick, strong plastic sheeting to create a cylindrical bag. The bags are then installed into dug pits and secured with a support structure to the walls and pit bottom. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Users can obtain the bags from the manufacturer, or from partner NGOs who have already purchased bags. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Toilet type Accessory Evacuation method Pour-flush or dry Storage conditions Pit Capacity (L) ~1400 L Time until emptying >10 years Design Specifications Each bag is designed to have a diameter of 1 meter, with a depth ranging up to 1.8 meter, dependent on the number of latrine users. In addition the plastic material used was chosen be strong enough to withstand the weight of waste when emptying a pit, and to have small enough pores to prevent bacteria and nitrate from seeping into the groundwater table. Technical Support Technical support is provided by the manufacturer. Replacement Components The Dutch Latrine Bag does not require any additional components so no replacement components are required. Replacement bags can be purchased from the manufacturer. Lifecycle At least 10 years Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The manufacturer specifies performance targets related to preventing the groundwater table from becoming polluted due to human waste in latrines, simplifying the latrine construction process, reducing latrine construction cost, and re-using waste for agricultural applications or as fertilizers. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety The bags were developed prevent human waste from contaminating the groundwater table. When bags are removed from pits, care must be taken to not handle the human waste. Complementary Technical Systems The bags are used together with constructed pits and existing latrines. Academic Research and References AAWS. Dutch Latrine Bag. Available: https://www.aaws.nl/en/dutch-latrine-bag/ Environmental Expert. AAWS- Dutch Latrine Bag. Available: https://www.environmental-expert.com/products/aaws-dutch-latrine-bag-332471 WEDC, 2014, Latrine pit excavation and linings , Loughborough University. Compliance with regulations Unknown Evaluation methods The bags are evaluated visually during installation to ensure that they are not torn and that they are secured to the bottom of the pit. Other Information The product appears to be discontinued with no updated information online as of 2018. Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment.
